issue.message: WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE is deprecated (and is not granted) when targeting Android 13+. If you need to write to shared storage, use the `MediaStore.createWriteRequest` intent.

READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE is deprecated (and is not granted) when targeting Android 13+. If you need to query or interact with MediaStore or media files on the shared storage, you should instead use one or more new storage permissions: `READ_MEDIA_IMAGES`, `READ_MEDIA_VIDEO` or `READ_MEDIA_AUDIO`.
Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background
Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background

A newer version of com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp than 4.12.0 is available: 5.4.0

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).
Avoid using "`px`" as units; use "`dp`" instead
Use `@RequiresApi(Build.VERSION_CODES.O) instead of `@TargetApi` to propagate the requirement to callers of `createNotificationChannel`
Use `@RequiresApi(Build.VERSION_CODES.LOLLIPOP) instead of `@TargetApi` to propagate the requirement to callers of `requiredPermissions`
Use `@RequiresApi(Build.VERSION_CODES.LOLLIPOP) instead of `@TargetApi` to propagate the requirement to callers of `onPermissionRequest`
Use `@RequiresApi(Build.VERSION_CODES.LOLLIPOP) instead of `@TargetApi` to propagate the requirement to callers of `shouldInterceptRequest`
Use `@RequiresApi(Build.VERSION_CODES.KITKAT_WATCH) instead of `@TargetApi` to propagate the requirement to callers of `shouldInterceptRequest`
Use `@RequiresApi(Build.VERSION_CODES.LOLLIPOP) instead of `@TargetApi` to propagate the requirement to callers of `shouldOverrideUrlLoading`
Use `@RequiresApi(21) instead of `@TargetApi` to propagate the requirement to callers of `getStatusBarColor`
Use `@RequiresApi(Build.VERSION_CODES.LOLLIPOP) instead of `@TargetApi` to propagate the requirement to users of `WebRtcPermissionsModel`
Use `ResourcesCompat.getDrawable()`
Use `ResourcesCompat.getDrawable()`
For locale "es" (Spanish) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
For locale "it" (Italian) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
For locale "fr" (French) the following quantity should also be defined: `many` (e.g. "1000000 de jours")
For locale "pt" (Portuguese) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
For locale "pt" (Portuguese) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
"boas vindas" is a common misspelling; did you mean "Boas-vindas"?
issue.errorLine1: <string name="onboarding_reshow">Mostrar novamente tela de boas vindas</string>

issue.message: "boas vindas" is a common misspelling; did you mean "Boas-vindas"?
issue.errorLine1: <string name="onboarding_reshow">Mostrar novamente ecrã de boas vindas</string>

issue.message: Inconsistent number of arguments in formatting string `message_permission_request`; found both 0 here and 2 in values/strings.xml
issue.errorLine1: <string name="message_permission_request">％1 $ s \ n \ n使用したい：\ n \ n％2 $ s</string>

issue.message: Inconsistent number of arguments in formatting string `message_permission_request`; found both 0 here and 2 in values/strings.xml
issue.errorLine1: <string name="message_permission_request">%1$s \ n \ n desea utilizar: \ n \ n% 2 $ s</string>

For language "ko" (Korean) the following quantities are not relevant: `one`
For language "zh" (Chinese) the following quantities are not relevant: `one`

The Network Security Configuration allows the use of user certificates in the release version of your app
Insecure Base Configuration

Permitting connections with SSL-related errors could allow eavesdroppers to intercept data sent by your app, which impacts the privacy of your users. Consider canceling the connections by invoking `SslErrorHandler#cancel()`.

Permitting connections with SSL-related errors could allow eavesdroppers to intercept data sent by your app, which impacts the privacy of your users. Consider canceling the connections by invoking `SslErrorHandler#cancel()`.
It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.

This `AsyncTask` class should be static or leaks might occur (com.cookiegames.smartcookie.reading.activity.ReadingActivity.LoadData)
issue.errorLine1: private inner class LoadData : AsyncTask<Void?, Void?, Void?>() {

issue.message: This tag and its children can be replaced by one `<TextView/>` and a compound drawable
Very long vector path (3552 characters), which is bad for performance. Considering reducing precision, removing minor details or rasterizing vector.
Set `android:baselineAligned="false"` on this element for better performance
Use a `layout_width` of `0dp` instead of `match_parent` for better performance
Use a `layout_width` of `0dp` instead of `match_parent` for better performance
Use a `layout_width` of `0dp` instead of `match_parent` for better performance
Nested weights are bad for performance
